status: internal build workflow, not buyer-facing copy
purpose: Define the repeatable terminal workflow for building vertical diagnostic packs from the same SpecShift core engine.

workflow_steps.name: Select vertical pack
workflow_steps.description: Choose one registered vertical pack from the registry. Do not build multiple packs in the same pass.

workflow_steps.name: Define buyer workflow
workflow_steps.description: State the concrete workflow, buyer role, decision owner, expected inputs, systems touched, and consequences of silent failure.

workflow_steps.name: Map relevant failure families
workflow_steps.description: Select only the v0.5 failure families that fit the workflow. Do not imply all 25 cases apply.

workflow_steps.name: Create vertical pack artifact
workflow_steps.description: Generate a JSON and TXT artifact with pack positioning, buyer pain, safe use cases, excluded uses, likely failure patterns, selected v0.5 cases, deliverables, fit gate, and failure column.

workflow_steps.name: Run claim-boundary review
workflow_steps.description: Check that the pack does not claim benchmark, certification, compliance, security, runtime, deployment, clinical/legal/HR decision-making, or safety guarantee status.

workflow_steps.name: Commit and index
workflow_steps.description: Validate JSON, update index, run claim scanner, and commit using claim-safe language.

standard_boundary_line: This vertical diagnostic pack is a pre-deployment diagnostic and architecture-stage review extension. It is not a benchmark, certification, compliance audit, runtime monitor, security tool, deployment approval, or guarantee of AI safety.
failure_column: This workflow fails if it creates vertical packs that are disconnected from the core product, overclaim domain validation, or suggest professional/regulatory assurance.
